name: Alternating Features
why_it_works: The zig-zag reading pattern (left image → right text, right image → left text) creates natural rhythm that keeps long-scroll pages engaging. The 5/7 asymmetric split gives the content column more space than the image, reinforcing that the visuals are supporting evidence, not the message. Alternating background colors (white → off-white) provide section breaks without explicit dividers, and the generous 4rem vertical padding gives each feature room to breathe at its own pace.
when_to_use: Product feature overviews with 3–5 distinct capabilities, SaaS marketing pages, product detail sections. Works best when each feature has a genuinely different visual and can be shown in isolation.
watch_out: Requires strong visuals for each section — placeholder boxes or generic screenshots flatten the effect. Also avoid more than 5 items; the pattern becomes repetitive and the rhythm turns monotonous.
